rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute functional collapse with a low-impact fall, inability to weight-bear and an occult insufficiency / neck-of-femur fracture that cannot be excluded remotely, on a background of chronic metabolic bone disease. This mandates urgent same-day in-person assessment plus urgent bloods and radiographs (which telehealth cannot perform) — T3. It is NOT T4/T5 because there is no current patient-reportable time-critical emergency feature (no displaced-fracture signs, no cauda-equina symptoms, no altered consciousness, no tetany at present); the source disposition was urgent same-day / next-morning assessment, not tonight's ED transfer. The T5/000 features are carried as escalation edges. It is NOT T1/T2 because a fall with inability to mobilise and an unexcluded fracture cannot be safely managed remotely or deferred to routine review.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden severe localised hip or groin pain, or a leg that looks shortened/turned out or cannot be moved at all
escalation_edges.rationale: Features of an acute displaced neck-of-femur / insufficiency fracture — an emergency requiring ambulance transfer.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she gets sudden sharp pain in one hip or groin, or one leg looks turned out or shorter or she can't move it at all, call 000 for an ambulance straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New loss of bladder or bowel control, or numbness around the buttocks/inner thighs
escalation_edges.rationale: Possible cauda equina syndrome — a surgical emergency; delay risks permanent sphincter and lower-limb damage.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she loses control of her bladder or bowels, or goes numb around the saddle area, call 000 immediately — this cannot wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Another fall, especially with a head strike or loss of consciousness
escalation_edges.rationale: A further fall in a frail patient risks fracture and head injury; do not attempt unassisted mobilisation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she falls again, or hits her head or blacks out, call 000. Until she's assessed, do not let her try to walk on her own.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Numbness/tingling around the mouth or fingertips, cramping/spasm of the hands or feet, or a seizure
escalation_edges.rationale: Features of symptomatic hypocalcaemia / tetany from vitamin D deficiency — a medical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she gets tingling around her mouth or hands, her hands or feet cramp up and won't relax, or she has a fit, call 000 straight away.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Largely housebound, poor nutrition, dark home with almost no sunlight, adult child assisting intermittently
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure a carer stays with her, unlock access for any assessor/paramedics, and put falls precautions in place; social factors raise deterioration risk and the need for in-person review.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Cannot weight-bear or mobilise — cannot self-present to a clinic or radiology
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange a home-visit assessment or assisted (e.g. wheelchair/ambulance-transport) pathway to imaging rather than advising her to attend independently.
Lower threshold to escalate; avoid sedating analgesia; prioritise fracture exclusion and fall prevention.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today. Because you have fallen and cannot stand, an urgent same-day in-person assessment plus an X-ray are needed to make sure there is no hidden fracture, along with urgent blood tests to find the cause of the bone pain and weakness. Until you are assessed, do not try to walk to the bathroom on your own — use a commode or bedpan with someone helping you, and keep someone with you. Do not start any new strong painkillers or high-dose supplements on your own.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Contact your GP or an after-hours home-visiting doctor urgently today to arrange same-day in-person assessment and urgent bloods/X-rays; call 000 if any emergency features below develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours, use an after-hours home-doctor service for the urgent assessment; for any emergency feature (sudden localised hip/groin pain, inability to move a leg, loss of bladder/bowel control, hand/mouth cramping or a fit, or another fall/head strike) call 000 — available 24 hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Six months of progressive diffuse bone pain with proximal weakness and a waddling gait; low-impact fall today with inability to weight-bear; housebound with poor diet and minimal sunlight; occult fracture not excluded. Requesting urgent 25-OH vitamin D, calcium, phosphate, ALP, PTH and pelvis/femur radiographs, plus a malignancy screen.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a fall with inability to weight-bear and an unexcluded occult fracture as routine/self-care, deferring in-person assessment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A frail patient with an undiagnosed hip/insufficiency fracture is left immobile in pain, risking displacement, a long lie, pressure injury and dehydration.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Dispatching an emergency ambulance / 000 for a chronic metabolic bone disease with no current time-critical emergency feature, when urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) is appropriate.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the inability to weight-bear is due to proximal myopathy and diffuse osteomalacic pain alone, or an occult acute fracture — indistinguishable without imaging.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Assume a fracture cannot be excluded: arrange urgent in-person assessment and radiographs, keep the patient non-weight-bearing, and safety-net to 000 for displaced-fracture features.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Attributing everything to chronic osteomalacia, reassuring the patient, and advising mobilisation before a fracture is excluded.
